Package algatorgraph

Class Edge<V,​W>

  • java.lang.Object
    • algatorgraph.Edge<V,​W>
  • All Implemented Interfaces:
    java.io.Serializable
    public class Edge<V,​W>
extends java.lang.Object
implements java.io.Serializable
    Represents an undirected edge in a graph data structure with generic identifiers for the two vertices. This class provides methods to get and set the edge's properties, including its vertices and weight. The order of the vertices does not affect equality, since the edge is undirected.

    The equals, hashCode, and toString methods have been overridden to ensure that two edges are considered equal if they connect the same vertices, regardless of order. 

     Usage example:
 
 UEdge<String, Integer> edge1 = new UEdge<>("A", "B", 5);
 UEdge<Integer, Integer> edge2 = new UEdge<>(1, 2, 5);
 System.out.println("Vertex 1: " + edge1.getVertex1());
 System.out.println("Vertex 2: " + edge1.getVertex2());
 System.out.println("Weight: " + edge1.getWeight());
    See Also:
    Serialized Form

    • Constructor Summary

      Constructors 
      Constructor Description
      Edge​(V vertex1, V vertex2)
      Constructs a new undirected edge with the specified vertices and no weight.
      Edge​(V vertex1, V vertex2, W weight)
      Constructs a new undirected edge with the specified vertices and weight.

    • Method Summary

      All Methods Instance Methods Concrete Methods 
      Modifier and Type Method Description
      boolean equals​(java.lang.Object o)
      Compares this edge to the specified object (Edge) for equality.
      V getVertex1()
      Returns the identifier of the first vertex of this edge.
      V getVertex2()
      Returns the identifier of the second vertex of this edge.
      W getWeight()
      Returns the weight of this edge.
      int hashCode()
      Returns a hash code value for this edge.
      void setVertex1​(V v1)
      Sets the identifier of the first vertex of this edge.
      void setVertex2​(V v2)
      Sets the identifier of the second vertex of this edge.
      void setWeight​(W weight)
      Sets the weight of this edge.
      java.lang.String toString()
      Constructs a string representing this edge

    • Constructor Detail

      • Edge

        public Edge​(V vertex1,
            V vertex2,
            W weight)
        Constructs a new undirected edge with the specified vertices and weight.
        Parameters:
        vertex1 - the identifier of one vertex of the edge
        vertex2 - the identifier of the other vertex of the edge
        weight - the weight of the edge

      • Edge

        public Edge​(V vertex1,
            V vertex2)
        Constructs a new undirected edge with the specified vertices and no weight.
        Parameters:
        vertex1 - the identifier of one vertex of the edge
        vertex2 - the identifier of the other vertex of the edge

    • Method Detail

      • getVertex1

        public V getVertex1()
        Returns the identifier of the first vertex of this edge.
        Returns:
        the first vertex ID

      • getVertex2

        public V getVertex2()
        Returns the identifier of the second vertex of this edge.
        Returns:
        the second vertex ID

      • getWeight

        public W getWeight()
        Returns the weight of this edge.
        Returns:
        the weight of the edge

      • setVertex1

        public void setVertex1​(V v1)
        Sets the identifier of the first vertex of this edge.
        Parameters:
        v1 - the new identifier for the first vertex

      • setVertex2

        public void setVertex2​(V v2)
        Sets the identifier of the second vertex of this edge.
        Parameters:
        v2 - the new identifier for the second vertex

      • setWeight

        public void setWeight​(W weight)
        Sets the weight of this edge.
        Parameters:
        weight - the new weight for the edge

      • equals

        public boolean equals​(java.lang.Object o)
        Compares this edge to the specified object (Edge) for equality. Two edges are considered equal if they connect the same vertices (in any order) and have the same weight (including null weights).
        Overrides:
        equals in class java.lang.Object
        Parameters:
        o - the object to compare with this edge
        Returns:
        true if the given object represents an edge equivalent to this one, false otherwise

      • hashCode

        public int hashCode()
        Returns a hash code value for this edge. The hash code is computed based on the sum of the hash codes of the two vertices (order-independent) and the weight. This ensures consistency with the equals(Object) method.
        Overrides:
        hashCode in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        a hash code value for this edge

      • toString

        public java.lang.String toString()
        Constructs a string representing this edge
        Overrides:
        toString in class java.lang.Object
        Returns:
        the string value for this edge
